name: United States Virgin Islands (United States)

colors.symbolism: the field, representing the cleanliness and purity of the islands
the various flowers found on the islands and their golden warmth
fidelity, allegiance, and the surrounding seas

description: A white field bearing a simplified version of the United States coat of arms: a yellow American bald eagle with a U.S. shield on its breast, holding three arrows in its left talon and a green laurel branch in its right. The letters 'V' and 'I' in blue flank the eagle on the hoist and fly sides respectively.
history: Adopted on 17 May 1921. Designed by Percival Wilson Sparks, a sign painter, and his wife and her sister, the flag was first hoisted at the inauguration of Rear Admiral Sumner Kittelle as Governor of the Virgin Islands. The territory had been purchased from Denmark in 1917.

features.symbolism: displayed eagle holding three arrows in one talon and a green laurel branch in the other; on its breast a shield with the United States stars-and-stripes; representing the United States, the laurel for victory and peace, the three arrows for the three main islands (Saint Thomas, Saint John, and Saint Croix).

features.role: the letters 'V' and 'I' flanking the eagle
